title: scanner.rs: in-memory tree/witnesses state diverges from DB on unexpected scan_block failure
description: scanner.rs:400-437 — scan_block appends commitments to self.tree and updates self.witnesses in-memory throughout scan_tx, then calls save_block_state at the end. If save_block_state fails (I/O error, DB full), scan_block returns Err, but self.tree and self.witnesses have already been mutated for this block's outputs. The scan tip in the DB is not advanced (correct), but the in-memory state is now ahead of what the DB knows. On the next scan_to_tip call, scan_block replays the same block: scan_tx attempts to re-insert notes (hitting UNIQUE constraints, handled), but also re-appends each output's cmu to self.tree and updates all witnesses again. This double-appends those commitments to self.tree, producing a tree root that is inconsistent with the chain's actual commitment tree. Subsequent witness validity checks and anchor comparisons in the tx builder will fail. Fix: save_block_state failure should either reset self.tree and self.witnesses to their pre-block state (requires snapshotting), or the scan should reload state from DB before retrying.
